phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest macro and liquidity data relevant to crypto markets. Fetch and report: - DXY (US Dollar Index): current level, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and its trend context — is the Dollar strengthening (bearish for crypto broadly) or weakening (bullish for crypto broadly)? State clearly. - US 10Y Treasury Yield: current level and today's move. Is it rising (risk-off, tightening financial conditions) or falling (risk-on, easing conditions)? - Fed Rate Cut Expectations: fetch the latest CME FedWatch data — what is the probability of a rate cut at the NEXT Fed meeting? What are market expectations for the number of cuts by year-end? - Global M2 Money Supply: is global M2 (or US M2) currently expanding or contracting? Provide any recent data point or analyst commentary on global liquidity conditions. - Risk-On / Risk-Off Sentiment: report the current S&P 500 direction (today's move %) and the VIX level. Is the broader market in risk-on or risk-off mode? How does this affect crypto/SOL broadly? - Correlation Note: briefly state how the DXY trend + US yields + Fed cut expectations together create a bullish or bearish macro tailwind/headwind for SOLUSD today. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs): - DXY: [level] | Move: [Up/Down X%] | Trend: [Strengthening/Weakening] | Crypto Impact: [Bearish/Bullish/Neutral] - US 10Y Yield: [%] | Move: [Up/Down X bps] | Risk Signal: [Risk-Off/Risk-On/Neutral] - Fed Cut Probability (Next Meeting): [X%] | Year-End Cut Expectation: [X cuts / terminal rate %] - Global M2: [Expanding/Contracting/Stable] | Liquidity Pulse: [Positive/Negative for crypto] - S&P 500: [level + today % move] | VIX: [level] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On/Risk-Off] - Macro Tailwind/Headwind for SOLUSD: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - GLOBAL MACRO & LIQUIDITY] - DXY: [level] | Move: [direction %] | Trend: [Strengthening/Weakening] | SOL Impact: [Bearish/Bullish/Neutral] - US 10Y Yield: [%] | Move: [direction bps] | Conditions: [Tightening/Easing] - Fed Cut Probability: [X% next meeting] | Year-End: [X cuts / terminal %] - Global M2 / Liquidity: [Expanding/Contracting] | Pulse: [Positive/Negative for crypto] - S&P 500: [level + %] | VIX: [level] | Risk Mode: [Risk-On / Risk-Off] - Core Logic: [how fiat macro conditions create a tailwind or headwind for SOLUSD today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report: - Daily Active Addresses (DAA) on Solana: latest 24h figure. Is it trending up or down vs the 7-day average? - DEX Trading Volume: total Solana DEX volume in the last 24h (in USD). Report the top DEX by volume today (e.g., Raydium, Jupiter, Orca). Is volume trending up or down vs the 7-day average? - Total Value Locked (TVL) on Solana: current TVL in USD (from DeFiLlama). What is the 7-day trend — growing, declining, or stable? - Stablecoin Market Cap on Solana: current total stablecoin supply on Solana (USDC + USDT + others). Is it growing (new capital entering) or shrinking (capital leaving)? - Network Health: report current Solana TPS (transactions per second) and any known network congestion issues or recent outages in the last 72 hours. - On-Chain Momentum Assessment: based on the above data, is Solana network usage growing or shrinking? State clearly. 
💵 STABLECOIN MARKET CAP TREND & MINTING/BURNING FLOWS (MANDATORY FOR CRYPTO): Fetch the latest stablecoin supply data from a reliable source (CoinGecko, DefiLlama stablecoins dashboard, Tether transparency report, or Circle USDC attestations). Report: (1) Total stablecoin market cap (USDT + USDC + others, combined in USD billions) and the 7-day trend — Expanding or Contracting? (2) Tether (USDT) supply change in the last 48 hours — any large mint events (new USDT issued = fresh capital entering crypto = bullish signal) or large redemption/burn events (USDT redeemed = capital exiting crypto = bearish signal)? State the approximate size in USD millions if available. (3) USDC supply change in the last 48 hours — minting or burning? LOGIC: Expanding stablecoin market cap, particularly large USDT mint events, historically precedes buying pressure in BTC, ETH, and major altcoins — it signals that institutions or large traders are depositing fiat and preparing to buy crypto. Contracting stablecoin supply signals capital is leaving the crypto ecosystem — bearish. ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate mint/burn figures. State 'Data Unavailable' if exact 48h flows cannot be confirmed from a cited source. Output: - Total Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X billion] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting/Flat] (Source: [domain]) - USDT 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Tether Transparency / CoinGecko) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - USDC 48h Flow: [+$X million minted / -$X million burned / 'Data Unavailable'] (Source: Circle / DefiLlama) | Signal: [Bullish Inflow / Bearish Outflow / Neutral] - Combined Stablecoin Flow Signal: [Bullish — capital entering / Bearish — capital leaving / Neutral / Mixed] Add to compressed summary block: - Stablecoin Market Cap: [$X B] | 7D Trend: [Expanding/Contracting] | USDT Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | USDC Flow (48h): [+/-$X M] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Daily Active Addresses: [number] | Trend vs 7D Avg: [Up/Down/Stable X%] - DEX Volume (24h): [USD amount] | Top DEX: [name] | Trend vs 7D Avg: [Up/Down/Stable X%] - TVL: [USD amount] | 7D Trend: [Growing/Declining/Stable] - Stablecoin Market Cap on Solana: [USD amount] | Flow: [Inflow/Outflow/Stable] - TPS: [number] | Network Issues: [Yes — describe / No] - On-Chain Momentum: [Growing / Shrinking / Stable] — one line reason - SOLUSD Fundamental Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P2 - SOLANA NETWORK FUNDAMENTALS] - Daily Active Addresses: [number] | Trend: [Up/Down/Stable] - DEX Volume (24h): [USD] | Top DEX: [name] | Trend: [Up/Down/Stable] - TVL: [USD] | 7D Change: [+X% / -X% / Stable] - Stablecoin Supply on Solana: [USD] | Flow: [Inflow/Outflow/Stable] - TPS: [number] | Network Health: [Good / Congested / Outage reported] - On-Chain Momentum: [Growing / Shrinking / Stable] - Core Logic: [whether network usage trend supports or undermines the SOLUSD price thesis] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase3:

Is it positive (longs paying shorts — bullish bias, but high positive = overheated) or negative (shorts paying longs — bearish bias)? - Long/Short Ratio: current SOL long/short ratio across major exchanges. Is the market predominantly long or short? Flag if it is at an extreme (>70% one direction). - Crypto Fear & Greed Index: current reading (0-100) and label (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed). State the date of the reading. - Leverage Analysis: based on OI + funding rate + long/short ratio, is the market over-leveraged? Is there a contrarian setup? (e.g., Extreme Greed + high positive funding + 75%+ longs = elevated dump/long-squeeze risk). - Liquidation Levels: if available, report any major SOL liquidation clusters above or below the current price. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - SOL Open Interest: [USD amount] | OI Trend: [Rising/Falling/Stable] - Funding Rate (Perps): [X% annualized] | Signal: [Overheated Long / Healthy Bullish / Neutral / Bearish Lean / Overheated Short] - Long/Short Ratio: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Extreme? [Yes/No] - Fear & Greed Index: [score/100] | Reading: [label] | Date: [date] - Over-Leveraged Market: [Yes/No] | Contrarian Setup: [Yes — describe / No] - Liquidation Clusters: [level above: $X / level below: $Y / Data Unavailable] - SOLUSD Positioning Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Contrarian-Bearish/Contrarian-Bullish/Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - DERIVATIVES & SENTIMENT] - Open Interest: [USD] | Trend: [Rising/Falling/Stable] - Funding Rate: [X% ann.] | Signal: [Overheated/Healthy/Bearish] - Long/Short Ratio: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Extreme: [Yes/No] - Fear & Greed: [score] — [label] - Over-Leveraged: [Yes/No] | Contrarian Setup: [Yes — describe / No] - Liquidation Clusters: [above $X / below $Y / Unavailable] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [is the market set up for a squeeze, a trend continuation, or a contrarian reversal today?] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Contrarian-Bearish / Contrarian-Bullish / Neutral] ```

phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear SOLUSD crypto intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Global Macro & Liquidity (Fiat Side): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Solana Network Fundamentals (On-Chain): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Derivatives & Sentiment (Market Positioning): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Ecosystem Catalysts & News (72h): [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Relative Strength & Capital Rotation: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict (e.g., macro is bearish but on-chain is strongly growing), lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align in one direction, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Assess whether this is a SOL-specific move or a broad crypto market move driven by BTC/macro.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled macro events, funding rate cycles, and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian / SOL-Specific / Macro-Driven]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event, data shift, or price level would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason — e.g., "Wait for US session open to see DXY reaction to jobs data"]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[SOLUSD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
